title: Protocol: Cross-sectional registry analysis of geriatric clinical trial equity across seven age-related conditions in Africa
body: This cross-sectional registry study will quantify the geriatric clinical trial evidence void for Africa's aging population. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for interventional studies matching geriatric and elderly-related terms across ten African countries, the United States, Japan, and the United Kingdom. Seven age-related conditions will be examined: dementia and Alzheimer disease, osteoporosis, falls prevention, urinary incontinence, polypharmacy, frailty, and cataracts. The primary outcome is the Geriatric Evidence Void ratio per condition computed as the comparator-to-Africa trial count ratio. Secondary analyses include geriatric trials per million elderly population using United Nations estimates for population aged sixty and over, condition-country heatmaps identifying systematic zero-trial cells, and country-level geriatric trial access rankings. Japan is included as the world's most aging-focused research nation for maximal contrast. Broad geriatric queries combining age-related terms will provide overall landscape counts. All queries will be scripted in Python with twenty-four-hour cached results. Limitations include single-registry restriction, variable geriatric terminology, possible undercount of Japanese trials registered on domestic registries, and population estimates that aggregate diverse aging trajectories across the continent.

sentences.text: This cross-sectional registry study will quantify the geriatric clinical trial evidence void for Africa's rapidly aging population across seven conditions that define the elderly disease burden.

We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for interventional studies matching geriatric and elderly terms across ten African countries and three comparators including Japan as the world's most aging-focused research nation.
The primary outcome is the Geriatric Evidence Void ratio per condition, computed as the comparator-to-Africa trial count ratio for each of seven age-related conditions.
Secondary analyses include geriatric trials per million elderly population, condition-country heatmaps identifying systematic zero-trial cells, and country-level geriatric trial access rankings.
United Nations 2024 estimates for population aged sixty and over will be used for per-capita calculations across the ten African countries and comparator regions.
All queries will be scripted in Python with 24-hour cache validity and results archived alongside the generated HTML dashboard for full reproducibility.

Limitations include single-registry restriction, variable geriatric terminology, possible undercount of Japanese trials on domestic registries, and population estimates aggregating diverse aging trajectories.

outsideNote.data: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, 10 African countries, US + Japan + UK, 7 geriatric conditions

ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
